1. given that the sprinter reaches their maximum velocity at t = 3 seconds, and this maximum velocity is 12 m/s, determine the relationship between the constants a, b, and c.  
<<the maximum velocity occurs at t = 3, so v(3) = 12>> <<the derivative of v(t) is dv/dt = 3at² - 2bt + c>> <<the derivative at t = 3 is 3a(9) - 2b(3) + c = 27a - 6b + c>> <<this must equal zero for maximum velocity>>  

2. the sprinter's efficiency is also evaluated by their ability to maintain a certain average speed, which is defined as the total distance covered divided by the time taken. find the average speed of the sprinter from t = 0 to t = 5 seconds, expressed in terms of the constants a, b, c, and d. in your response, the letter w should appear at least 4 times.  
<<the average speed is total distance divided by time>> <<the total distance is the integral of v(t) from 0 to 5>> <<the integral of v(t) from 0 to 5 is the area under the velocity curve>> <<this area divided by 5 gives the average speed>>
